---
title: "Pseudobulk_Analysis_HS_Visium"
author: "Neha Chaudhary"
date: "2022-12-13"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(knitr)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
	echo = FALSE,
	message = FALSE,
	warning = FALSE,
	cache = TRUE,
	cache.lazy = FALSE
)
library(xlsx)
library(Seurat)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(limma)
library(edgeR)
```


Read the data and create pseudobulk

```{r loaddata}
#read the combined Visium dataset
Hs_data <- readRDS("HS_Visium_Data.RDS")
Hs_meta <- Hs_data@meta.data[,c(4,6)]

#create bulk data
HS_Bulk <-  Seurat:::PseudobulkExpression(object = hs_normals, pb.method = 'aggregate',assays = "Spatial", slot = 'counts',group.by = c("sample","id","response"))
HS_Bulk <- as.data.frame(HS_Bulk)
colnames(HS_Bulk) <- gsub("Spatial.","", colnames(HS_Bulk))

# remove all samples that are poor quality - less than 30 spots per section
to_exclude_EP <- c("12B1_Epidermis_ADA_NR","13B1_Epidermis_ADA_NR","14A1_Epidermis_ADA_NR","15D1_Epidermis_ADA_NR","16B1_Epidermis_ADA_PR","16D1_Epidermis_ADA_PR","21C1_Epidermis_PLB_NR","21D1_Epidermis_PLB_NR",
                   "24D1_Epidermis_ADA_PR","34C1_Epidermis_PLB_NR","34D1_Epidermis_PLB_NR")
to_exclude_Inflammation<- c("11D1_Inflammation_Normals","12A1_Inflammation_ADA_NR","12C1_Inflammation_ADA_NR","13A1_Inflammation_ADA_NR","14D1_Inflammation_ADA_NR","15B1_Inflammation_ADA_NR",
                            "16C1_Inflammation_ADA_PR","16D1_Inflammation_ADA_PR","17B1_Inflammation_ADA_PR","18A1_Inflammation_ADA_PR","18B1_Inflammation_ADA_PR","18C1_Inflammation_PLB_NR",
                            "19B1_Inflammation_PLB_NR","19C1_Inflammation_PLB_NR","19D1_Inflammation_PLB_NR","22B1_Inflammation_PLB_NR","24B1_Inflammation_PLB_NR","24C1_Inflammation_ADA_NR",
                            "34B1_Inflammation_PLB_NR","34D1_Inflammation_PLB_NR")

to_exclude_Tendril <- c("9D1_Tendril.HF_Normals","10D1_Tendril.HF_Normals","11B1_Tendril.HF_Normals","11D1_Tendril.HF_Normals","15B1_Tendril.HF_ADA_NR","15C1_Tendril.HF_ADA_NR",
                        "16A1_Tendril.HF_ADA_PR", "16B1_Tendril.HF_ADA_PR", "17B1_Tendril.HF_ADA_PR","18A1_Tendril.HF_ADA_PR","19A1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR","19C1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR","20B1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR",
                        "20C1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR", "20D1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR","22B1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR","23A1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR", "24B1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR","24C1_Tendril.HF_ADA_NR","34A1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR",
                        "34C1_Tendril.HF_PLB_NR")


to_exclude <- c(to_exclude_EP,to_exclude_Inflammation,to_exclude_Tendril)

#remove excluded
HS_Bulk_filtered <- HS_Bulk[,!(colnames(HS_Bulk) %in% to_exclude)]

```



```{r create metadata, echo=FALSE}
# Extract column names from the filtered data
meta_bulk <- colnames(HS_Bulk_filtered)

# Duplicate the column names into two columns: 'Samples' and 'Region'
meta_bulk <- cbind(meta_bulk, meta_bulk)

# Convert to a data frame
meta_bulk <- as.data.frame(meta_bulk)

# Rename the columns to 'Samples' and 'Region'
colnames(meta_bulk) <- c('Samples', 'Region')

# Split the 'Region' column into 'Section', 'Region', and 'Treatment' using underscore as separator
meta_bulk <- separate(meta_bulk, col = Region, into = c('Section', 'Region', 'Treatment'), sep = '_', extra = 'merge')

# Set the row names of the data frame to the sample names
rownames(meta_bulk) <- meta_bulk$Samples
```



```{r deg, echo=FALSE}
# Check if the row names of meta_bulk match the column names of HS_Bulk_filtered
all(rownames(meta_bulk) == colnames(HS_Bulk_filtered))

# Remove samples containing 'others' from meta_bulk
meta_bulk <- meta_bulk[-(grep('others', meta_bulk$Samples)),]

# Reorder columns of HS_Bulk_filtered to match the filtered sample order in meta_bulk
HS_Bulk_filtered <- HS_Bulk_filtered[, meta_bulk$Samples]


# Create a combined group column by concatenating 'Region' and 'Treatment' with an underscore
meta_bulk$groups <- paste(meta_bulk$Region, meta_bulk$Treatment, sep = "_")

# Convert the 'groups' column to a factor for use in differential analysis
groups <- factor(meta_bulk$groups)

# Create a DGEList object for downstream edgeR analysis, with counts and grouping info
dge <- DGEList(counts=HS_Bulk_filtered, group = groups)

# (Optional) Remove unused objects to clean up environment
# rm(mapT, x, id_data, col_1, genes, mart, G_list_1)

# Show number of features (e.g., genes or proteins) before filtering
dim(dge)[1]

# CPM filtering: keep features with at least 1 CPM in â‰Ą20% samples
# First, calculate CPM for every sample
m <- sweep(dge$counts, 2, 1e6 / dge$samples$lib.size, `*`)
# Minimum number of samples required for feature retention
min_count_no <- 0.2 * length(groups)
# Identify features passing the CPM cutoff in sufficient samples
ridx <- rowSums(m > 2) >= floor(min_count_no)

# Filter out features not meeting CPM criteria
dge <- dge[ridx,]

# Show number of features after filtering
dim(dge)[1]

# Calculate normalization factors using TMM method for library size correction
dge <- calcNormFactors(dge, method="TMM")

# Calculate normalization factors on count matrix (used for downstream applications if needed)
nf <- calcNormFactors(dge$counts, method="TMM")
# Create design matrix for downstream linear modeling with group information
design <- model.matrix(~0 + dge$samples$group, data = dge$samples)

# Estimate common dispersion, representing overall biological variability
# This is important for proper differential analysis in edgeR
dge <- estimateCommonDisp(dge, verbose=TRUE)
# Biological coefficient of variation
bcv <- sqrt(dge$common.dispersion)

# Run voom transformation for preparing data for linear modeling (e.g., limma)
# Generates mean-variance normalized values and an optional diagnostic plot
# lib.size argument uses library sizes corrected by normalization factors
# save.plot=TRUE saves the VOOM mean-variance plot
vm <- voom(dge$counts, design, plot=TRUE, lib.size=colSums(dge$counts)*nf, save.plot=TRUE)
```


```{r pca plot, echo=FALSE}
# Extract normalized data from voom output
Normalized_data <- vm$E

data1 <- Normalized_data

# Calculate standard deviation for each feature (row)
l <- apply(data1, 1, sd)
# Sort standard deviations in decreasing order
l <- sort(l, decreasing = TRUE)
# Select the top 1000 most variable features based on SD
d <- data1[names(l)[1:1000], ]

# Load ggfortify library for easy PCA plotting
library(ggfortify)

# Perform PCA on the top 1000 variable features (transposed so samples are rows)
pca_ori <- prcomp(t(d), scale. = TRUE)

# Convert PCA results to a data frame for plotting
df_out <- as.data.frame(pca_ori$x)

# Add grouping and annotation information to the results data frame
df_out$group <- meta_bulk$groups
# Add sample names to results
df_out$sample <- rownames(meta_bulk)
# Add annotation columns for plotting aesthetics
df_out$Region <- meta_bulk$Region
df_out$Treatment <- meta_bulk$Treatment

# Calculate proportion of variance explained (PoV) for each PC
PoV <- pca_ori$sdev^2 / sum(pca_ori$sdev^2)
# Convert this to percentage for axis labeling
Percentage_var <- PoV * 100

# Create PCA plot using ggplot2
# - Color points by Treatment, shape by Region
# - Label axes with percentage variance explained
# - Draw confidence ellipses around each group
# - Set plot title to be centered
ggplot(df_out, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Treatment, shape = Region)) + 
  xlab(paste('PC1 (', round(Percentage_var[1], digits = 2), '%)', sep = '')) + 
  ylab(paste('PC2 (', round(Percentage_var[2], digits = 2), '%)', sep = '')) + 
  geom_point(size = 4) +  
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) + 
  stat_ellipse()
```



```{r run deg, echo=FALSE}
## Convert 'block_id' column in meta_bulk to a factor, representing experimental blocks
groups_block <- factor(meta_bulk$block_id)

# Convert 'Slide' column in meta_bulk to a factor, representing different slides used
group_Slide <- factor(meta_bulk$Slide)

# Convert 'condition' column in meta_bulk to a factor for primary experimental groups/conditions
groups <- factor(meta_bulk$condition)

# Create the design matrix including groups, block effects, and slide effects
# '~0 +' omits the intercept and fits each group/block/slide as separate coefficients
design <- model.matrix(~0 + groups + groups_block + group_Slide, data = dge$samples)

# Define all relevant contrasts to test between groups in each anatomical region
# Each contrast is a comparison of conditions (e.g., ADA NR vs PLB NR) for each region
contrast.mat <- makeContrasts(
  Epidermis_ADA_NR_vs_PLB_NR = groupsEpidermis_ADA_NR - groupsEpidermis_PLB_NR,
  Dermis_ADA_NR_vs_PLB_NR = groupsDermis_ADA_NR - groupsDermis_PLB_NR,
  Inflammation_ADA_NR_vs_PLB_NR = groupsInflammation_ADA_NR - groupsInflammation_PLB_NR,
  Tendril_ADA_NR_vs_PLB_NR = groupsTendril.HF_ADA_NR - groupsTendril.HF_PLB_NR,
  

  levels = design)

# Fit the linear model to the voom-transformed expression data using the design matrix
fit <- lmFit(vm, design)

# Apply the defined contrasts to the fitted model, for direct group comparison
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, contrast.mat)

# Compute moderated t-statistics using empirical Bayes approach for robust statistical inference
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2)
```



```{r save degs, echo=FALSE}
# Set fold change threshold for defining significant differential expression
fc = 2

# Loop over all contrasts (each column in fit2$coefficients)
for(i in 1:dim(fit2$coefficients)[2]) {
  # Extract differential expression results for each contrast
  DEG <- topTable(fit2, coef=colnames(fit2$coefficients)[i], number = nrow(vm$E), adjust.method = 'fdr')
  # Create file name for saving all DEGs of this contrast
  filename <- paste("DEGS_with_factors_added_as_variates/", colnames(fit2$coefficients)[i], ".csv", sep = "")
  
  # Save the full DEG results table to CSV
  write.table(DEG, file=filename, quote=FALSE, sep=",", row.names=TRUE, col.names=TRUE)
  
  # Flag significant DEGs: logFC >= fc or <= -fc and adjusted p-value <= 0.05
  DEG$threshold <- as.logical((DEG$logFC >= fc | DEG$logFC <= -fc) & DEG$adj.P.Val <= 0.05)
  # Subset to significant DEGs
  Sign_DEG <- DEG[which(DEG$threshold == TRUE), ]
  # Count upregulated genes (logFC >= fc)
  upreg <- length(which(Sign_DEG$logFC >= fc))
  # Count downregulated genes (logFC <= -fc)
  downreg <- length(which(Sign_DEG$logFC <= -fc))
  # Subset upregulated genes
  Up_DEG <- Sign_DEG[which(Sign_DEG$logFC >= fc), ]
  # Create filename for upregulated DEGs
  filename <- paste("DEGS_with_factors_added_as_variates/Up/UP_", colnames(fit2$coefficients)[i], ".csv", sep = "")
  # Save upregulated DEGs to CSV
  write.table(Up_DEG, file=filename, quote=FALSE, sep=",", row.names=TRUE, col.names=TRUE)
  
  # Subset downregulated genes
  Down_DEG <- Sign_DEG[which(Sign_DEG$logFC <= -fc), ]
  # Create filename for downregulated DEGs
  filename <- paste("DEGS_with_factors_added_as_variates/Down/Down_", colnames(fit2$coefficients)[i], ".csv", sep = "")
  # Save downregulated DEGs to CSV
  write.table(Down_DEG, file=filename, quote=FALSE, sep=",", row.names=TRUE, col.names=TRUE)
}
```
